title: draba bryoides var. bryoides
intro: Draba bryoides var. bryoides, also known as Draba bryoides, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a small, annual or biennial plant that is native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers and succulent leaves. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Draba bryoides is typically prepared as a tea or salve, with the leaves being harvested in the summer months and dried for later use in herbal preparations.
